name: Russia–Ukraine War
status: Active large-scale conventional warfare; front lines contested across eastern Ukraine
summary: Russia launched large-scale military operations in Ukraine on February 24, 2022, initiating the largest ground conflict in Europe in decades. Fighting continues along a roughly 1,000-km front line in eastern and southern Ukraine. Multiple Western nations have provided military and financial support to Ukraine; Russia has conducted aerial strikes on Ukrainian infrastructure. Both sides report significant casualties.

events.description: Russian forces conducted overnight missile strikes on energy infrastructure in Kharkiv and Odesa regions, according to Ukrainian officials.
Ukrainian military reported positional advances near Robotyne in Zaporizhzhia oblast following armored operations.
G7 nations agreed on a new tranche of financial support for Ukraine totaling $50 billion.

name: Israel–Gaza War
status: Active military operations in Gaza; large-scale displacement and humanitarian access constraints reported
summary: On October 7, 2023, Hamas-led armed groups conducted an attack inside Israel, killing approximately 1,200 people and taking around 250 hostages. Israel subsequently launched a military campaign in the Gaza Strip. The conflict has resulted in large numbers of casualties on both sides and significant displacement of civilians. Ceasefire negotiations and hostage release talks have continued in multiple rounds of international mediation.

events.description: Israeli military operations continued in southern Gaza. Aid organizations reported food access constraints in northern Gaza.
Mediation talks in Doha did not produce an agreement; differences over hostage release sequencing were cited.
UN Security Council passed a resolution calling for immediate humanitarian pauses; the United States abstained.

name: Sudan Civil War
status: Active conflict between SAF and RSF; large-scale displacement ongoing
summary: Armed conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) began in April 2023 and has expanded across multiple regions of Sudan. The conflict has displaced over 8 million people and famine conditions have been reported in multiple areas, according to UN agencies. Multiple rounds of internationally mediated talks have not produced a sustained ceasefire.

events.description: RSF forces were reported to have seized additional territory in North Darfur; UN agencies confirmed famine conditions in El Fasher.
African Union-mediated talks in Nairobi concluded without a ceasefire agreement.

name: Myanmar Civil War
status: Multi-front civil conflict; armed opposition groups have gained territory in multiple regions
summary: Following a military takeover in February 2021, Myanmar has experienced ongoing civil conflict between the armed forces (Tatmadaw) and multiple opposition groups including the People's Defence Force (PDF) and ethnic armed organizations (EAOs). Armed opposition groups have captured significant territory, including several major towns, since 2023.

events.description: The Three Brotherhood Alliance reported capturing Lashio city in Shan State from military forces.
UN Special Rapporteur documented airstrikes on civilian settlements in Sagaing region.

name: Iran–Israel–US War
globalImpact: Airspace closures across Iraq, Iran & Jordan · Oil prices volatile · Strait of Hormuz at risk · Multiple analysts warn of wider regional war
status: Active direct military exchanges between Iran and Israel; US forces engaged; regional proxies active across 6 countries
summary: Direct military exchanges between Iran and Israel have occurred multiple times since April 2024, representing a significant escalation from decades of indirect conflict. Iran launched large-scale drone and ballistic missile attacks directly at Israeli territory; Israel conducted retaliatory strikes on Iranian soil. The United States has deployed additional carrier strike groups to the region and participated in air defense operations. Iran-affiliated armed groups remain active in Lebanon (Hezbollah), Iraq, Syria, and Yemen (Houthi), creating a multi-front pressure environment. Iran's nuclear enrichment has advanced to near-weapons-grade levels, raising the prospect of further preemptive action. Multiple governments have issued travel advisories, airspace over Iraq and Iran has seen repeated closures, and global oil markets have experienced significant price volatility due to Strait of Hormuz risk. Analysts across multiple institutions have described this as the highest risk of broader regional war since the 1973 Arab-Israeli conflict.

events.description: Iraqi and Jordanian civil aviation authorities issued NOTAMs closing portions of their airspace following reported missile activity over the region.
Israel's military reported intercepting an Iranian ballistic missile salvo over the Negev desert; Iran's IRGC confirmed the launch.
IAEA reported Iran has enriched uranium to 84% purity at the Fordow facility, one step below the 90% weapons-grade threshold.
Oil prices rose 6% in a single session on reports of Iranian naval exercises near the Strait of Hormuz, through which approximately 21% of global oil supply transits.
Israel conducted airstrikes on weapons depots in Syria attributed to Hezbollah supply lines, according to Israeli military statements.
The United States announced additional sanctions on Iran's oil and drone sectors; a second carrier strike group arrived in the Eastern Mediterranean.
Multiple European and Asian airlines suspended flights over Iraqi airspace for 72 hours following a missile warning bulletin.
Hezbollah and Israeli forces exchanged fire across the Lebanon border; Lebanese health authorities reported civilian casualties in southern Lebanon.

name: India–Pakistan Border Tensions
status: Elevated tensions along Line of Control; diplomatic relations at reduced levels
summary: India–Pakistan relations remain strained following an armed attack in Jammu & Kashmir that India attributed to Pakistan-based armed groups. Cross-border firing incidents along the Line of Control (LoC) have been reported. Both nuclear-armed states have reduced diplomatic representation. The underlying territorial dispute over Kashmir remains unresolved.

events.description: The Indian Army reported cross-border firing incidents in the Poonch sector; one soldier was reported wounded.
Pakistan's Foreign Ministry summoned the Indian High Commissioner over alleged violations.

name: Haiti Security Crisis
status: Armed groups control significant areas of Port-au-Prince; UN-authorized security mission deployed
summary: Haiti has experienced a deteriorating security situation with armed groups, including the Viv Ansanm coalition, controlling large areas of Port-au-Prince and restricting humanitarian access. The UN Security Council-authorized Multinational Security Support Mission (MSS) led by Kenya began deployment in 2024. No functioning elected government is currently in place.

events.description: MSS forces and Haitian National Police conducted a joint security operation in Cité Soleil.
WFP reported that supply lines to northern Haiti have been disrupted by armed group checkpoints; approximately 300,000 people face acute food insecurity.

name: South China Sea Disputes
status: Recurring naval incidents; multiple overlapping maritime territorial claims
summary: Multiple states, including China, the Philippines, Vietnam, and Taiwan, maintain overlapping territorial and maritime claims in the South China Sea. Incidents between Chinese Coast Guard vessels and Philippine supply ships near Scarborough Shoal and the Second Thomas Shoal have been reported with increasing frequency. The United States has conducted Freedom of Navigation Operations (FONOPs) in the area.

events.description: Chinese Coast Guard vessels used water cannons against Philippine civilian supply vessels near Second Thomas Shoal, according to Philippine authorities.
The US Navy conducted a Freedom of Navigation Operation through the Taiwan Strait; PLA Navy vessels tracked the operation.

name: Kosovo–Serbia Tensions
status: Periodic security incidents in northern Kosovo; EU-mediated normalization talks ongoing
summary: Kosovo declared independence from Serbia in 2008, a declaration Serbia has not recognized. Tensions remain elevated, particularly in the Serb-majority northern municipalities. EU-facilitated normalization dialogue has continued but implementation of the Brussels Agreement has stalled. NATO's KFOR peacekeeping mission remains deployed in Kosovo.

events.description: A security incident occurred in Mitrovica between Kosovo Police and protesters; KFOR deployed additional units.
An EU envoy conducted shuttle diplomacy between Belgrade and Pristina; no agreement was reached.

name: Yemen–Houthi Conflict
status: Houthi maritime attacks in Red Sea ongoing; US and UK airstrikes on Yemen continue
summary: Houthi forces (Ansar Allah) began conducting attacks on commercial shipping in the Red Sea in November 2023, citing the Israel-Gaza conflict as justification. These attacks have disrupted shipping routes through the Red Sea and Bab el-Mandeb Strait, prompting many operators to divert around the Cape of Good Hope. US and UK forces have conducted airstrikes on Houthi military infrastructure in Yemen. The broader Yemeni civil war, ongoing since 2014, continues.

events.description: Houthi forces launched a ballistic missile toward the Israeli port city of Eilat; the missile was reported intercepted by Israel's Arrow defense system.
US CENTCOM reported airstrikes on Houthi missile storage facilities near Sanaa.
Shipping company Maersk announced continued Red Sea diversions via the Cape of Good Hope; freight costs are approximately 40% higher year-over-year.
